mnemonics.title: Half Adder Truth Table
mnemonics.content: SUM = A XOR B, CARRY = A AND B. Remember: 'Sum is X-OR, Carry is AND' - like 'Some Addicts CRy' (S= XOR, C= AND)

mnemonics.title: Full Adder Sum Output
mnemonics.content: Sum = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin. Mnemonic: 'Silly Xylophone Tuner' (Sum = XOR of all three inputs)

mnemonics.title: Full Adder Carry Output
mnemonics.content: Cout = (A·B) + (Cin·(A⊕B)). Remember: 'Can I Buy Or Carry?' (C = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B)))

mnemonics.title: 2's Complement Subtraction
mnemonics.content: A - B = A + (2's complement of B). Rule: 'Invert all bits, then add 1' - N = (~N) + 1

mnemonics.title: Adder-Subtractor Mode Control
mnemonics.content: When M=0: Circuit acts as Adder. When M=1 (with XOR gates): Circuit acts as Subtractor. Remember: 'M-0 = Add, M-1 = Minus'

mnemonics.title: Ripple Carry Adder Flow
mnemonics.content: Each Full Adders Cout → next FA's Cin. Remember: 'RCA Ripples, Carry Propagates' - like falling dominoes

mnemonics.title: Overflow Detection
mnemonics.content: Overflow = Cout(n-1) XOR Cout(n). Mnemonic: 'OVerflow = XOR of Last Two Carries'

acronyms.usage: Adds 2 single-bit numbers, produces Sum and Carry. No carry input.
Adds 3 single-bit numbers (A, B, Cin), produces Sum and Carry out. Used in multi-bit addition.
Cascaded full adders where carry propagates from LSB to MSB. Simple but slow (propagation delay).
Fast adder that computes carries in parallel using Generate and Propagate signals.克服了RCA的传播延迟问题.
M=0 for addition, M=1 for subtraction in unified adder-subtractor circuit.
Cin is input carry to a stage, Cout is output carry to next stage. Critical for multi-bit addition.
Method to represent signed numbers. For subtraction A-B: compute 2's comp of B and add to A.
Gi = Ai · Bi. When Generate=1, carry is produced regardless of input carry.
Pi = Ai ⊕ Bi. When Propagate=1, input carry is passed to output carry.

visualTips.visualization: Draw two boxes side by side: LEFT box = XOR gate (outputs Sum), RIGHT box = AND gate (outputs Carry). Label inputs A and B entering both boxes.
Picture TWO LEVELS: First level = two XOR gates (A⊕B, then result⊕Cin), Second level = one AND gate + one OR gate for carry. Think of it as 'XOR-XOR for Sum, AND-OR for Carry'.
Visualize falling DOMINOES: FA1 triggers FA2, FA2 triggers FA3... The carry 'ripples' left to right (LSB to MSB). Each FA waits for previous carry - like waiting in a queue.
Picture MUX-style: Each input B passes through XOR gate with Mode M. When M=1, B becomes ~B (inverted). Then add 1 using Cin=1. Result: A + (~B) + 1 = A - B in 2's comp.
Think of FLIP + ADD: Write number → flip ALL bits (0→1, 1→0) → add 1 at LSB. Visual: Mirror image horizontally, then add 1 at the right end.

Imagine PARALLEL processing: Instead of waiting for carry to ripple, pre-compute ALL possible carries using Generate (G) and Propagate (P) in a tree structure. Think of it as 'express elevator' vs 'stairs'.

analogies.analogy: Like adding SINGLE-DIGIT numbers in primary school (e.g., 3+5=8, no carrying to next column). Only two inputs, simple result.
Like adding multi-digit numbers with carrying. You need the previous carry (Cin) to correctly compute the current digit - just like adding 7+8=15, you 'carry 1' to next column.
Like a relay race where each runner (Full Adder) must wait to receive the baton (carry) before running. The total time = sum of all runners' times. Slow for long distances.

Like using 'compliments' in shopping: To give change for $50 on a $23 purchase, you count UP from $23 to $50 ($7 + $20 + $10 + $10). The complement method finds the difference by 'adding to reach' a base.
Like a DUAL-PURPOSE calculator: One button (M) switches between 'ADD' mode and 'SUBTRACT' mode. The internal circuits reconfigure based on the mode - like a multi-language translator changing modes.
Like a SUPERMARKET EXPRESS LANE: Instead of waiting in a long queue (ripple), multiple cashiers (parallel logic) check all customers at once and decide who goes to which lane. Much faster!
